reasoning:
The forecast asks if the US State Department's Diversity Visa instructional page ("Submit an Entry") will be active at a specific URL on July 1, 2025. It is currently May 20, 2025, leaving roughly 6 weeks until resolution. 
As of today, the page is live. The Trump administration has openly criticized the Diversity Visa (DV) lottery and has expressed a desire to terminate it. However, the DV program was established by the Immigration Act of 1990, meaning its elimination requires an act of Congress. While an executive order could potentially halt processing, an outright legal repeal within the next 6 weeks is highly improbable given the speed of the legislative process. 

Even if the administration attempts to undermine the program administratively, permanently deleting the instructional page on an active government domain before the program is legally dissolved is unlikely. We saw earlier in 2025 that the administration scrubbed federal websites of "Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion (DEI)" content. Crucially, the Diversity Visa page survived this January/February purge because the program is a statutorily mandated immigration pathway, not an internal DEI policy, despite sharing the word "Diversity."

Users and legal professionals online are still actively discussing the future of the program and upcoming cycles (like DV-2026/2027), indicating the program framework is currently intact. Because the base rate for 6-week URL persistence is very high, and because no specific threat to remove this exact page immediately has materialized despite the administration's stance, I estimate an 80% probability that the URL will remain online on July 1, 2025.
